meaning: n.[鍙瞉澶у悕(鏃ユ湰灏佸缓鏃朵唬鐨勫ぇ棰嗕富)
examples: The Daimyo knew the price: whoever rode the Kirin had to die.
As the boy's condition grew worse, the Priestesses of Shalassa told the Daimyo his son would not survive the night.
Historically, the samurai serving a daimyo, or feudal lord, would all wear similar armor, while here the mannequins sport different styles.
Mr Ishihara's photograph was of daimyo (lords') mansions.
